response:
Recorded as `docs/decisions/0035-tiered-agent-execution-defer.md`. Summary of the decision:

**Do not adopt tiered-agent execution for the Do phase now.** ISSUE-41's own pre-registered 4-arm test (ADR 0024's requirement) has never been run — I confirmed no `benchmarks/*tiered*` directory and no tiered-orchestration script exist on disk. Adopting the design on the strength of the idea alone would be exactly the "adoption on assertion" ADR 0024 forbids.

Rather than leave it stalled, the ADR resolves the three owner blockers from Comment 3 so the test can actually run next: accept the Sonnet-grades-Sonnet grader residual (per ADR 0019's precedent), accept tool-deny as inapplicable to a tool-using task (record it as an uncontrolled variable, don't block on it), and confirm the 3-cycle retry cap / plain-text validator feedback already pre-registered. It also notes #53/#54 (the `model:` omission gap) are already closed via ADR 0029, so the new script gets that gate for free.

The adopt/reject call itself is deferred to the 4-arm test's verdict — this ADR is written to be rationalized in place once that test produces a result, per the repo's "no second ADR to amend a sibling" rule.

One process note: I couldn't run `adr-lint` or any `node` command in this sandbox (all invocations were blocked as requiring approval), so I verified frontmatter, char budget (5936/6000), ADR cite resolution, and the falsifiable-criterion tag manually against the linter's source instead of via the actual gate — worth re-running `adr-lint` yourself before merge.
